title: Workload Tier Map
description: Schema for `.loa.config.yaml::workload_tier_map` (cycle-112 FR-3). Configures which model dispatch tier (advisor / executor / headless) each skill uses, with rationale and evidence references. Drift-gated in CI via .github/workflows/workload-tier-map-drift.yml (cycle-112 FR-4).

schema_version.description: Semantic-version-shaped string. Major bumps signal breaking schema changes (Phase B/C); minor bumps add backward-compatible fields.

tier.description: Default tier applied to any dispatching skill not enumerated in entries. Operators SHOULD enumerate exhaustively (cycle-112 FR-3 AC); the default is a quality-floor safeguard.

entries.description: Per-skill tier mapping. Skill names are the keys (e.g., '/review-sprint', 'bridgebuilder-review'). Drift gate (FR-4) requires every mutation to carry a Tier-Change-Evidence: or Operator-Approval: trailer in the PR body.

rationale.description: Free-text justification for the tier assignment. Cite empirical evidence (A/B benchmark, PR comparison, prior bench result) or 'default' for unevidenced entries.

evidence_ref.description: Pointer to the evidence backing the rationale. Recognized prefixes: 'memory:<file>' (auto-memory artifact), 'pr:<url>' (GitHub PR), 'default' (no specific evidence — used the workload_tier_map.defaults.tier), 'operator-decision:<NOTES.md anchor>', 'grimoire:<path>', 'kf:<KF-NNN>'.

tier.description: Workload tier. 'advisor' = highest-quality reasoning models (Opus, gpt-5.5-pro, gemini-3.1-pro). 'executor' = mid-tier (Sonnet, gpt-5.5). 'headless' = cheapest task-only models (claude-headless, codex-headless, gemini-headless).
